nameMeanings.name: Harington
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Harrington
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the three places called Harrington (Cumberland, Lincolnshire, Northamptonshire). The Cumberland placename derives from the Old English personal name Hæfer + Old English connective -ing- + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The Lincolnshire placename derives from the Old English personal name Hearra + Old English connective -ing- + tūn. The Northamptonshire derives from an Old English personal name Hǣthhere + Old English connective -ing- + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Herendeen .
Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hArrachtáin ‘descendant of Arrachtán’, a personal name from a diminutive of arrachtach ‘mighty, powerful’.
Irish: in Kerry, this name was adopted as an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó hIongardail, later Ó hUrdáil, ‘descendant of Iongardal’, a personal name of uncertain origin.

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Harington was born in 1407, in Hornby Castle, Lancashire, England, her father, Sir William Harington of Farleton, was 41 and her mother, Lady Margaret Neville, was 52. She married Alexander Radcliffe in 1438, in Salford, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. She died about 1490, in Salford, Lancashire, England, at the age of 84, and was buried in Manchester Cathedral, Manchester, Lancashire, England.

Elle a épousé Alexander Radcliffe en 1438, à Salford, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée environ 1490, à Salford, Lancashire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 84 ans, et a été enterrée à Manchester Cathedral, Manchester, Lancashire, Angleterre.

Sie hat 1438 in Salford, Lancashire, England Alexander Radcliffe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Sie starb um 1490 im Alter von 84 Jahren in Salford, Lancashire, England und wurde in Manchester Cathedral, Manchester, Lancashire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Haringtonが1407年にイギリスランカシュアイアホールンビー カストルで生まれた時，父Sir William Harington of Farletonは41歳，母Lady Margaret Nevilleは52歳であった。1438年にイギリスランカシュアイアサルフォードでAlexander Radcliffeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は約1490年にイギリスランカシュアイアサルフォードで84歳で死去し，イギリスランカシュアイアマンチェスターマンチェスター カスエドラルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Harington(이)가 1407년 영국 랭카실르 호른비 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William Harington of Farleton(은)는 41세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Margaret Neville(은)는 52세였다. 그녀는 1438년 영국 랭카실르 살퍼드에서 Alexander Radcliffe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 대략 1490년 영국 랭카실르 살퍼드에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 랭카실르 먼체스터 먼체스터 캐테드럴에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Harington родилась в 1407 г. в местности хаурнби кастл, ланкашир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William Harington of Farleton было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Margaret Neville, 52. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Alexander Radcliffe в 1438 г. в местности салфорд, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла около 1490 г. в местности салфорд, ланкашир, Англия в возрасте 84 лет и была похоронена в местности манчестир кэтедраль, манчестир, ланкашир, Англия.
